message:
fix: length-delimited codec frame alignment when num_skip < header_len

Fix a bug where the decoder would wait for the wrong number of bytes when
num_skip is set to less than the header length. Previously, after skipping
num_skip bytes, the decoder transitioned to DecodeState::Data(frame_len)
where frame_len is just the payload length. But when num_skip < header_len,
the retained header prefix bytes are part of the returned frame, so the
decoder needs to wait for retained_len = (header_len + frame_len) - num_skip
bytes instead.

Also add overflow checking:
- checked_add for header_len + frame_len to catch malicious length fields.
- checked_sub for total_frame_len - num_skip to catch misconfiguration
  where num_skip exceeds the total frame length.

New tests:
- builder_num_skip_zero_retains_entire_header_and_keeps_alignment: with
  num_skip=0, two consecutive 1-byte payloads decode correctly with their
  full 4-byte headers intact, proving frame boundary alignment is correct.
- builder_partial_header_retention_returns_remaining_prefix_bytes: with
  num_skip=2 on a 4-byte header, the frame includes the last 2 header
  bytes plus the 3-byte payload.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
